title: A Conserved Acetyl Esterase Domain Targets Diverse Bacteriophages to the Vi Capsular Receptor of Salmonella enterica Serovar Typhi
summary: The paper describes a diverse set of Vi phages targeting the Vi capsular antigen of Salmonella enterica serovar Typhi, belonging to the Myoviridae, Siphoviridae, and Podoviridae families. These phages were isolated from various geographic regions and clinical samples, with distinct plaque morphologies and genome characteristics. They share a conserved acetyl esterase domain in their tail spike proteins, essential for recognizing the Vi capsule. The phages exhibit varying degrees of genomic similarity to known phages like T7, SP6, and K1F, and some possess multiple tail spike proteins.

Vi phage I.isolation_source: clinical samples of stools from patients with typhoid fever
Vi phage I.morphology: isometric icosahedron-shaped head, ∼88 nm from apex to tail joint, ∼90-nm width, tail ∼115 nm in length, ∼18 nm in diameter, complex tail fiber structure
Vi phage I.growth_medium: Luria broth or agar plates at 37°C supplemented with aromatic amino acids
Vi phage I.similarity_percent: remarkable synteny with Shigella phage phiSboM-AG3

extra.genome_accession: related to GenBank accession number NC_013693

Vi phage III.isolation_source: clinical samples of stools from patients with typhoid fever
Vi phage III.morphology: icosahedral capsid with a diameter of ∼60 nm, short tails

Vi phage IV.isolation_source: clinical samples of stools from patients with typhoid fever
Vi phage IV.morphology: icosahedral capsid with a diameter of ∼60 nm, short tails
Vi phage IV.similarity_percent: most clearly resembles Salmonella phage SP6

Vi phage V.morphology: icosahedral capsid with a diameter of ∼60 nm, short tails
Vi phage V.similarity_percent: 90% with respect to Vi phages VI and VII

Vi phage VI.plaque_morphology: 0.3- to 0.4-cm plaques, consistently the largest and strikingly clear centers
Vi phage VI.morphology: icosahedral capsid with a diameter of ∼60 nm, short tails
Vi phage VI.similarity_percent: 95% with respect to Vi phage VII

Vi phage VII.morphology: icosahedral capsid with a diameter of ∼60 nm, short tails
Vi phage VII.similarity_percent: 95% with respect to Vi phage VI
